name: HiveTerm

runner.notes: Prepare a run worktree, open it in HiveTerm, provide prompt.md exactly, then run harness verification.

pricing_model: Commercial desktop product with free/pro tiers; model/agent subscriptions are bring-your-own where applicable.
public_stack_signals: Official site states Rust + Tauri desktop app around 20MB.
Built-in MCP server called Queen exposes orchestration tools.
Config-as-code workspace is centered on hive.yml.
moat_hypotheses: MCP-centered agent spawning and project-isolated sub-agent tree

sources.notes: Product positioning as multi-agent terminal workspace with MCP/Queen model. Refresh 2026-07-21: official site lists v0.37.0 and current macOS, Windows, and Linux downloads; agents page still frames multi-agent terminal orchestration.
Supported first-class agents and role/preset behavior.
Queen MCP server and project-isolated tool behavior.
Feature comparison claims around config-as-code, live tree, dev stack, diff/PR, voice, localization.

research.positioning: Terminal where AI agents work as a team, with a live split-pane grid and MCP-based sub-agent tree.

packaging: desktop app for macOS, Windows, Linux
data: pins and notes managed through MCP
unknowns: Internal UI framework and persistence implementation are not public in official docs.

infrastructure: Project-isolated sub-agent access through built-in MCP.
Runs dev servers, workers, and agents in one workspace.

agent_support: Aider through hosted terminal-like agent support
orchestration: Agents spawn sub-agents through Queen MCP
workspace_isolation: Sub-agents only see their own project
review_shipping: Inline diff, commit, and PR are claimed in comparison pages
remote_mobile: No mobile app identified in official sources reviewed

parity_risks: MCP sub-agent behavior may not be exercised by single-agent v0.1 tasks.
Review/PR features need GitHub-backed tasks to compare fairly.

hypotheses: MCP-native nested agent model is the core differentiator.
Rust/Tauri footprint and terminal-first UX appeal to developers who do not want a full IDE.
Config-as-code stack management could make repeated benchmark setup reproducible.
evidence: Official site documents Queen MCP with 20 tools and project-isolated sub-agents.
Official site states Rust + Tauri and cross-platform desktop support.
unknowns: Actual implementation quality of review, commit, and PR surfaces requires hands-on run.
